title: Heart surface anatomy
body_html:
The diagrams below show the surface markings of the heart and the orientation of the heart chambers

Image illustrating borders of the heart

Image sourced from Wikipedia

The college expects you to appreciate the cardiac structures making up the borders and surfaces of the normally-orientated heart. It is worth remembering the 4 corners of the heart (see image above and table below)

BorderStructureLandmarks
Right borderRight atrium
  • Upper: 3rd right costal cartilage
  • Lower: 6th right costal cartilage
Left borderLeft ventricle & left atrial appendage
  • Upper: 2nd left costal cartilage
  • Lower: 5th left ICS midclavicular line (apex)
Superior borderBoth atria & roots of great vessels
  • Right: 3rd right costal cartilage
  • Left: 2nd left costal cartilage
Inferior borderRight ventricle & apex of left ventricle
  • Right: 6th right costal cartilage
  • Lower: 5th left ICS midclavicular line (apex)

In 3D terms the heart is sometimes described as being a pyramid structure with the apex pointing down and to the left.

Image illustrating heart surfaces


SurfaceStructure
Sternocostal (anterior)Right ventricle (2/3) and right atrium (1/3)
Diaphragmatic (inferior surface)Right and left ventricle
Posterior (base of pyramid)Left atrium
Pulmonary (left)Left ventricle
Pulmonary (right)Right atrium

Image illustrating surface heart valves & auscultation sites


ValveAuscultation siteSurface marking / anatomical position
Aortic2nd ICS right sternal edge3rd ICS retrosternally
Pulmonary2nd ICS left sternal edge3rd rib left sternal edge
Tricuspid4th ICS left sternal edge4th ICS right sternal edge
Mitral5th ICS left midclavicular line4th rib left sternal edge

Image illustrating basic anatomy of the heart

Image sourced from Wikipedia
